author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Status update (2026-06-22).

`69d5e1b` (`agent-ready`) closed. `jjf ready` is live: walks
open issues, drops anything blocked by an open dependency,
excludes the roadmap, and sorts by type priority (bug >
feature > research > epic > unspecified) then `created_at`
FIFO. Flags: `--label` (AND), `--type` (OR), `--limit`,
`--json`. Dangling dep ids tolerated (a deleted id can't ever
close, so blocking forever would wedge progress).

Commit `594e257`. 231/231 workspace tests green (was 212;
ten new storage tests + nine new CLI integration tests).

`docs/cli-json.md` has the new `ready` envelope; `CLAUDE.md`
Queries section gains the canonical invocations.

This was the headline primitive this epic exists for. Open
under this epic next: `--claim` (atomic assign-and-go),
`remember`, MCP exposure of `ready` as a tool, and the
stale `subagent-working-a-git-bug-issue` skill rewrite.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Status update (2026-06-22).

Four child tickets filed after a beads (`reference/beads/`)
walkthrough. Beads is a Dolt-backed issue tracker for AI
workflows; these tickets borrow specific design ideas that
fit jjforge's shape.

- `81db913` `agent-remember` (feature) ŌĆö project-scoped
  persistent memory primitive. `jjf remember "<insight>"` /
  `jjf memories` / `jjf recall <key>` / `jjf forget <key>`.
  Memories ride the `issues` bookmark as a new file family;
  scalar LWW under the existing resolver. Surfaced at
  session start via `jjf show roadmap --include-memories`.
  Beads ref: `reference/beads/cmd/bd/memory.go`.

- `c3cc807` `agent-claim-atomic` (feature) ŌĆö `jjf update
  <id> --claim` atomically sets assignee + advances status
  to a new `in-progress` variant in one multi-op commit.
  Race-free for parallel agents. Adds `--include-claimed`
  to `jjf ready`. Beads ref: `bd update --claim`.

- `b6d066b` `agent-dep-types` (feature) ŌĆö extend dep edges
  from one kind (blocks) to four kinds (blocks /
  parent-child / related / discovered-from). Add `jjf dep
  add|rm|tree` verbs. `jjf ready` honors a fixpoint
  cascade through `parent-child`. Beads ref:
  `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:193-201` and
  `internal/storage/issueops/blocked_consistency.go`.

- `08bc9eb` `agent-await-gates` (research) ŌĆö design call
  on whether to ship beads-style external-signal gates
  (timer, gh:pr, gh:run, human). Output is a design doc
  + recommendation, not implementation. Beads ref:
  `internal/types/types.go` (`await_type` / `await_id`).

Suggested order: `agent-remember` and `agent-claim-atomic`
land first (small, independent, immediate agent-loop value).
`agent-dep-types` next (moderate, spec bump). `agent-await-
gates` is research-only; depending on recommendation, a
follow-up implementation ticket gets filed.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
QA red-team round 2026-06-25 closed.

Summary:
- sev:data-loss: 0 findings
- sev:wrong-answer: 1 finding (`121f48b` ŌĆö dep add: mixed-kind cycle (blocks + parent-child) silently accepted, locks both issues out of ready)
- sev:panic: 0 findings
- sev:contract-drift: 2 findings (`de59159` ŌĆö ls/ready exit 0 when format-version sentinel points at blob instead of commit; `88e4d6b` ŌĆö push_rejected --json message embeds raw git stderr with version-dependent hint text)
- total: 3 tickets filed under label `qa-redteam-2026-06-25`

Calibration disclosure: the plan's calibration band was 8ŌĆō15 findings; "<5 ŌåÆ likely under-attacking". This round landed 3 findings ŌĆö below the band. Honest interpretation: the data-loss surface is well-covered by the prior two QA rounds (round-1: 4 findings, round-2: 13 findings) and the harm-class attack tree mostly confirmed existing defenses hold. The proptest follow-up exists specifically because property-based generation can find counter-examples the harm-class tree won't. A future round using the proptest harness is the right vehicle to stress-test the surface more deeply.

Follow-up:
- `df74809 qa-proptest-harness` ŌĆö proptest harness against
  jjf-storage mutate/read surface. Filed as a feature, not blocking.

Repro harness: `experiments/qa-redteam-2026-06-25/` (scripts +
README finding index; `.scratch/` is gitignored).

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Five `qa-proptest-followup` tickets filed 2026-06-27, completing the
deferred work captured in `df74809`'s closing comment:

- `c6aed85` `proptest-multi-issue-generator` (feature) ŌĆö foundation; multi-stage generator that creates N issues then drafts dep edges across real ids. Unblocked.
- `6ce795c` `proptest-list-ready-monotone` (feature) ŌĆö Property 4. Closing only adds, opening only removes. Would have caught `121f48b`. Blocked by `c6aed85`.
- `1078439` `proptest-cycle-rejection` (feature) ŌĆö `add_dep_edge` never lands a `Blocks`/`ParentChild` cycle, including mixed-kind. Catches the regression class behind `121f48b`/`43c7615`. Blocked by `c6aed85`.
- `9f113b5` `proptest-list-ids-cardinality` (feature) ŌĆö Property 5. `list_ids().len() == N` after N creates. Unblocked, lower-leverage.
- `932cc40` `proptest-memory-surface` (feature) ŌĆö round-trip + idempotence + non-interference over `Storage::set_memory`/`unset_memory`/`read_memory`. Unblocked.

All five sit under `epic:agent-ergonomics` + shared label
`qa-proptest-followup`. `jjf ready --label qa-proptest-followup`
returns three (#1, #4, #5); the other two surface once the generator
lands. No roadmap reordering ŌĆö these are additive harness work, not a
priority shift.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
MCP server dropped from scope 2026-06-28. Reasoning recorded in the
body's Status section: the CLI already covers every real agent loop
(`--json` everywhere, <60ms `ready` warm, clap-driven help, harness
allowlisting). MCP would have been a second surface to maintain
without a consumer asking for it. YAGNI ŌĆö re-file if a real MCP-only
agent ever shows up.

No `ergo-mcp-server` ticket was ever filed, so no ticket to close.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
qa-proptest-followup cluster closed 2026-06-28. All five tickets
shipped over a single afternoon's serial-dispatch arc:

- `c6aed85` proptest-multi-issue-generator ŌĆö commit `c8a4b7a0`.
- `9f113b5` proptest-list-ids-cardinality ŌĆö commit `6f2594d4`.
- `932cc40` proptest-memory-surface ŌĆö commit `747bce18`.
- `6ce795c` proptest-list-ready-monotone ŌĆö commit `47eca167`.
- `1078439` proptest-cycle-rejection ŌĆö commit `fd2268dc`.

Harness wall-clock end-of-round: ~28s release for the full file at
default 16 cases. Workspace test count 583 ŌåÆ 625 (+42).

Two contract-clarification surprises the property tests flushed out:
- `unset_memory` on a missing key returns typed `Error::Invalid`
  ("no memory with key ..."), not silently-Ok. Codified by the
  memory-idempotence property.
- `add_dep_edge` cycle rejection uses typed `Error::DependencyCycle`
  / `Error::SelfDependency`, not the generic `Error::Invalid` the
  follow-up ticket assumed. Property matches the real contract.

The cycle property cross-checks storage's verdict with an
independent in-test DFS walker, so a regression in storage's
private `find_blocking_cycle` doesn't go undetected via cache
re-use. Would have falsified `121f48b` (mixed-kind cycle) and
`43c7615` (single-kind cycle) directly.

No follow-ups filed. The stretch goal "concurrent two-writer
lost-update cycle simulation" is parked; revisit only if a real
regression surfaces.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Subagent-skill rewrite landed 2026-06-28. Did not need a ticket ŌĆö
the work was a surgical edit to
`~/.claude/skills/subagent-working-a-jjforge-issue/SKILL.md`, not a
codebase change.

Three stale pockets removed, one new section added:

- **Removed** `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST=1` block + the `git symbolic-ref
  HEAD refs/heads/main && git reset --hard main` HEAD-recovery
  dance. Both went obsolete with the v3 storage cutover (writes via
  `refs/jjf/*` plumbing; HEAD never moves; no env-var opt-in).
- **Removed** the "Forgot JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST" row from Common
  Mistakes (stale by transitivity).
- **Tidied** the "two things on the `issues` bookmark" framing ŌĆö
  now reads "two things on the issue", since the bookmark is no
  longer the user-facing surface for writes.
- **Added** an "Actor attribution" section codifying the
  `JJF_ACTOR` env var + `--actor` flag chain (precedence: flag >
  env > `jj user.name`). Parallel subagents share the same
  `jj user.name`; the actor override is how the eventual reader
  tells them apart. Also added a corresponding Common Mistakes
  row for "actor was set but the comment landed under jj
  user.name anyway."

Net: ~15 lines changed. Recipe, REQUIRED steps, Boundary, and most
of Common Mistakes untouched ŌĆö they were correct and useful.

The epic body's "child tickets we'll file" list mentions
`ergo-subagent-skill`. No ticket was filed and none is needed; the
skill now matches current jjforge reality.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Follow-up tickets from the final whole-branch review of the
2026-06-28 drop-epic-label feature (commits 852f2a38..5690c905):

- `b417864` `new-parent-slug-broken` (bug) ŌĆö `jjf new --parent
  <slug>` fails with `bad_id`; only 7-hex ids work. Discovered
  while filing the other follow-ups (couldn't use slug to
  attach them to this epic). The migration script was
  unaffected because it pre-resolved slugs to ids.
- `03df0f3` `parent-matches-option-ref` (feature) ŌĆö clippy-
  ergonomic refactor: `&Option<IssueId>` ŌåÆ `Option<&IssueId>`.
- `095d60c` `parent-flag-bad-hex-silent` (bug) ŌĆö `--parent
  <bad-7-hex>` silently matches nothing instead of exiting 1
  with `issue_not_found`. Footgun for typo'd ids.
- `956a8d5` `test-helpers-shared-module` (feature) ŌĆö dedupe
  `parse_id_from_stdout` (and other 2+ test helpers) into a
  shared `crates/jjf/tests/common/mod.rs`.
- `4fe0406` `drop-epic-label-migration-archive` (feature) ŌĆö
  design call: commit the operational migration script as an
  audit artifact, or leave it gitignored per the original spec.

All filed as children of this epic via `--parent 5a755ec`.
Two bugs above features per the bugs-before-features rule.

Most are Minor; the bug-class items (`b417864`, `095d60c`) are
worth queuing above the feature backlog when this epic's next
work round happens.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Two `--parent` follow-up bugs fixed this round, both filed from
the drop-epic-label final review.

- `b417864 new-parent-slug-broken` closed (commit `fbf66a82`).
  `run_new` now uses `resolve_handle` for `--parent`, matching
  `ls`/`ready`/`search`. Storage open reordered to be available
  before the resolver.
- `095d60c parent-flag-bad-hex-silent` closed (commit `2ce23ddd`).
  `run_ls`/`run_ready`/`run_search` now do `storage.read` after
  the resolver so a well-formed-but-nonexistent hex id exits 1
  with `issue_not_found` instead of empty exit 0.

Workspace tests: 638 green (added 3 new CLI integration tests +
the 3 from b417864). No regressions.

Above-the-line backlog under this epic now: 3 open features
(`03df0f3`, `956a8d5`, `4fe0406`). No bugs remain.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Two more follow-ups landed:

- `03df0f3 parent-matches-option-ref` closed (commit `58446f9d`).
  Inline 3-line refactor; `ref_option` lint forestalled.
- `956a8d5 test-helpers-shared-module` closed (commit `c04126bc`).
  Moved 8 helpers (JJF_BIN, scratch, make_jj_repo,
  make_initialized_repo, run_jjf, run_jjf_with_stdin,
  parse_id_from_stdout unified to IssueId, parse_envelope) into
  `crates/jjf/tests/common/mod.rs`. 26 test files touched,
  -1497/+194 net. Subagent flagged `create_issue` (4 distinct
  signatures across files) as a future consolidation ŌĆö not
  filed yet.

638/638 workspace tests green. Above the line remaining:
`4fe0406 drop-epic-label-migration-archive` (user design call).
